  "findings": [
    "PVC flue pipe is back-pitched, preventing proper drainage of acidic condensate back to the furnace trap.",
    "Risk of condensate pooling in the vent pipe, which can restrict flue gas flow, cause pressure switch faults, or leak into unconditioned spaces."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down and lock out the furnace to prevent operation under unsafe venting conditions.",
    "Re-slope the PVC flue piping to ensure a minimum upward slope of 1/4 inch per foot toward the furnace, in accordance with manufacturer instructions and code."
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched condensing furnace flue violates code and manufacturer specifications. It leads to condensate accumulation, which can block the vent, cause safety switches to trip, or damage the equipment and surrounding structure.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54"
  ]
}
